title: Full Stack Developer
subtitle: Build complete web products across frontend, backend, APIs, databases, and deployment.
short_description: A product-focused engineering role for learners who enjoy owning complete features end to end.
description: Full Stack Developers design user interfaces, build backend APIs, model databases, implement authentication, deploy apps, and collaborate across product and engineering workflows.
demand_label: High demand across startups, service companies, and product teams
highlights: Strong portfolio role
Works well with AI-integrated SaaS projects
Maps directly to both undergraduate and graduate programs

payload.source: Roles Targeted + Full Stack curriculum outcomes

title: Frontend Developer
subtitle: Build responsive, interactive, accessible interfaces with modern JavaScript and React.
short_description: A UI engineering role for learners who enjoy product experience, components, and browser-side logic.
description: Frontend Developers translate product requirements into polished interfaces, reusable components, API-connected pages, and responsive user experiences.
demand_label: Steady demand in SaaS, product, and agency teams
opportunity_count_label: Strong entry path for web-focused learners
highlights: Great visual portfolio role
Clear upgrade path to full stack
Pairs well with UI/UX awareness

payload.source: Web Development and Full Stack Development + AI tracks

title: Backend Developer
subtitle: Design APIs, authentication, databases, services, and reliable server-side systems.
short_description: A systems and API role for learners who enjoy logic, data modeling, and scalable services.
description: Backend Developers build REST APIs, handle authentication, design schemas, manage integrations, and make application logic reliable and secure.
demand_label: High demand across product, fintech, SaaS, and backend-heavy teams
opportunity_count_label: Strong for learners with DSA and database depth
highlights: Strong path to system design
Good fit for product-company interviews

payload.source: Advanced DSA, Web Development, System Design outcomes

title: Data Analyst
subtitle: Use SQL, Excel, Power BI, and Python to turn business data into decisions.
short_description: A business-facing analytics role for learners who enjoy patterns, dashboards, and decision support.
description: Data Analysts clean data, write SQL, build dashboards, measure funnels/cohorts, and communicate insights to stakeholders.
demand_label: High demand in operations, sales, finance, SaaS, and product analytics
opportunity_count_label: Good fresher entry route into data careers
highlights: Fastest data entry path
Can grow into BI, data science, or analytics engineering

title: Data Scientist
subtitle: Build models, analyze patterns, evaluate predictions, and communicate ML-backed insights.
short_description: An applied ML role for learners who enjoy statistics, experimentation, and model-driven products.
description: Data Scientists perform EDA, create features, train models, evaluate metrics, explain trade-offs, and turn data into predictive systems.
demand_label: Strong demand for applied ML and AI-ready profiles
opportunity_count_label: Best with strong portfolio projects and ML fundamentals
highlights: Portfolio-heavy role
Pairs strongly with GenAI and RAG projects

title: ML Engineer
subtitle: Move ML models from notebooks into APIs, services, containers, and production workflows.
short_description: A bridge role between machine learning, backend engineering, and deployment.
description: ML Engineers package models, expose them through APIs, track experiments, optimize inference, and work with deployment and monitoring.
demand_label: Growing demand where AI products need production deployment
opportunity_count_label: Strong for learners with ML + backend + Docker projects
highlights: High-value AI production role
Pairs with Cloud/DevOps roadmap

payload.source: Graduate Track 03 and Track 06 AI deployment

title: Data Engineer
subtitle: Build reliable pipelines, warehouses, transformations, and cloud data platforms.
short_description: A production data role for learners who enjoy pipelines, SQL, cloud, and backend-style engineering.
description: Data Engineers build ETL pipelines, orchestrate workflows, design warehouses, transform data with dbt, and monitor cloud data systems.
demand_label: High demand as companies scale analytics and AI infrastructure
opportunity_count_label: Strong for SQL + Python + cloud pipeline profiles
highlights: Excellent route into data platform and AI infrastructure teams

title: Cyber Security Analyst
subtitle: Monitor threats, analyze vulnerabilities, investigate incidents, and document security findings.
short_description: A security operations role for learners interested in networks, systems, and defensive analysis.
description: Cyber Security Analysts understand networking, detect vulnerabilities, analyze logs, prepare reports, and support incident response workflows.
demand_label: Growing demand across SOC, fintech, SaaS, and managed security teams
opportunity_count_label: Good fresher path with lab proof and reports
highlights: Strong if paired with VAPT reports and controlled lab projects

title: Cloud Engineer
subtitle: Provision, deploy, secure, and monitor cloud infrastructure for production applications.
short_description: A cloud operations role for learners who like AWS, infrastructure, deployment, and reliability.
description: Cloud Engineers work with EC2, S3, RDS, VPCs, IAM, deployment automation, monitoring, and cloud cost/reliability decisions.
demand_label: High demand as teams move workloads and ML APIs to cloud platforms
opportunity_count_label: Strong with AWS project proof and deployment runbooks
highlights: Good bridge into DevOps, SRE, and MLOps

title: DevOps Engineer
subtitle: Automate builds, deployments, infrastructure, monitoring, and production operations.
short_description: A production engineering role for learners who enjoy automation, containers, cloud, and reliability.
description: DevOps Engineers create CI/CD pipelines, containerize apps, manage infrastructure as code, deploy services, and improve production reliability.
demand_label: High demand in SaaS, AI deployment, and microservices teams
opportunity_count_label: Strong with Docker, CI/CD, AWS, Kubernetes, and Terraform proof
highlights: Strong capstone value through deployment architecture and live pipelines

title: AI Engineer
subtitle: Build AI-powered features, RAG systems, agents, and LLM-integrated applications.
short_description: A future-ready product engineering role for learners combining software skills with LLM systems.
description: AI Engineers integrate LLM APIs, design prompts, build RAG workflows, add embeddings search, create AI agents, and deploy AI-backed applications.
demand_label: Fast-growing demand as AI becomes standard in product teams
opportunity_count_label: Strong if paired with full stack or ML project depth
highlights: Differentiator across all specialization tracks
Useful for software, data, security, and cloud careers

payload.source: AI Integration phase and AI Agents + MCP bonus module
